title: Unable to alter table to add primary
description:
### What happens?

Unable to alter a table to add primary key.
The documentation mentions it is supported though. May be i m missing something.
https://duckdb.org/docs/sql/statements/alter_table.html

### To Reproduce

```
~ $ duckdb
v1.1.3 19864453f7
Enter ".help" for usage hints.
Connected to a transient in-memory database.
Use ".open FILENAME" to reopen on a persistent database.

D CREATE TABLE integers (i INTEGER, j INTEGER);

D ALTER TABLE integers ADD PRIMARY KEY (i);
Not implemented Error: No support for that ALTER TABLE option yet!

D 
```

The below message is surprising
```
Not implemented Error: No support for that ALTER TABLE option yet!
```

### OS:

MAC OS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.3 19864453f7

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

MAC M1

### Full Name:

Basanth Jenu H B

### Affiliation:

Intuit

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE mixed_types (id INTEGER, name TEXT, created_at TEXT, is_active INTEGER);
INSERT INTO mixed_types (id, name, created_at, is_active) VALUES (1, 'Test User', '2023-10-01 10:00:00', 1);
CREATE TABLE integers (i INTEGER, j INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE integers_with_pk (i INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, j INTEGER);
SELECT * FROM mixed_types WHERE is_active IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: ALTER TABLE option for adding primary key not implemented
